curation.synopsis: Captain Uriel Ventris and the Ultramarines 4th Company are despatched to Tarsis Ultra, an industrial frontier world directly in the path of a Tyranid hive-fleet splinter pushing coreward through the Eastern Fringe. The defence is mounted jointly with the Mortifactors Chapter — an austere, death-cult-oriented Astartes brotherhood under Chaplain Astador whose doctrine of acceptable losses pulls hard against Ventris's instinctive Ultramarine pragmatism — and supported by Tarsis Ultra's local Imperial Guard militia, a substantial Adeptus Mechanicus contingent and a returning Inquisitor Ario Barzano. The campaign runs as a planet-scale siege procedural: hive-city perimeters, falling industrial belts, the slow grinding of Astartes-grade defensive lines against an enemy that cannot be deterred. The decisive choice the novel turns on is whether to deploy a Magos-engineered Vespasian-class biological weapon against the Hive Fleet — a Codex-Astartes violation the Mortifactors propose and Ventris ultimately authorises, ending the planetary defence at the cost of a death-oath that propels Dead Sky, Black Sun. Closes with Tarsis Ultra held and Ventris sentenced to a death-oath alongside Pasanius.
